entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The target crop for plant regeneration and genetic transformation protocols.

entities.name: PLANT REGENERATION
entities.description: A protocol for developing plants from cotyledonary node explants.

entities.name: GENETIC TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: The process of introducing foreign DNA into pigeonpea using Agrobacterium tumefaciens.

entities.name: COTYLEDONARY NODE EXPLANTS
entities.description: The specific plant tissue used as the starting material for regeneration.

entities.name: SHOOT INDUCTION MEDIUM
entities.description: Medium used to induce the development of shoot-buds from explants.

entities.name: MURASHIGE AND SKOOG MEDIUM
entities.description: The base nutrient medium used for plant tissue culture.

entities.name: BENZYLADENINE
entities.description: A cytokinin used in the shoot induction medium to promote shoot growth.

entities.name: SHOOT ELONGATION MEDIUM
entities.description: Medium used to promote the rapid elongation of developed shoots.

entities.name: GIBBERELLIC ACID-A
entities.description: A plant hormone used in the shoot elongation medium.

entities.name: INDOLE BUTYRIC ACID
entities.description: A plant hormone used to induce rooting in regenerated shoots.

entities.name: AGROBACTERIUM TUMEFACIENS
entities.description: The bacterium strain C-58 used to mediate genetic transformation.

entities.name: PCAMBIA1301
entities.description: A binary plasmid vector used to carry transgenes into the plant genome.

entities.name: BETA-GLUCURONIDASE
entities.description: A reporter gene used to confirm successful transformation.

entities.name: HYGROMYCIN
entities.description: An antibiotic used as a selection agent to identify transformed shoots.

entities.name: GUS ASSAY
entities.description: A biochemical test used to confirm the expression of the GUS transgene.

entities.name: PCR
entities.description: Polymerase Chain Reaction used to confirm the integration of transgenes.

entities.name: SOUTHERN BLOT HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: A molecular biology technique used to confirm stable transgene integration.
